key_insight: A mathematician-plus-AI workflow can produce a fully axiom-clean Lean 4 formalization of a major mean-field result for the Vlasov equation, including existence, uniqueness, stability, and the mean-field limit.
engineering_relevance: The paper suggests AI-assisted theorem proving can accelerate formal verification while also generating reusable mathematical libraries, especially in areas like optimal transport and PDE analysis.
method: Directed AI-assisted translation from LaTeX to Lean 4
Formalization game criteria: compile successfully, no `sorry`, and rely only on Lean's foundation
Reused the resulting optimal-transport layer as a self-contained Mathlib-compatible module
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a complete development with 299 declarations, 49 reusable declarations, a 22-declaration interface, and runtime of about a week for headline theorems and about a month for the full build.
watch_item: The results come from one case study, so the reported speed and reuse gains may not generalize across papers, toolchains, or theorem domains.
paper_title: A Formalization of the Mean-Field Derivation of the Vlasov Equation: AI-Assisted Lean Formalization as a Strategy Game
